source_ref.compose_env_source: existing 211 runtime env path, not copied or printed

review_notes: This entry records a reviewed Admin Runtime governance projection smoke for the 2384e19 Foundation Alpha POC runtime.

The operator verifier source was synced at 8206690 and classified as runtime-neutral; the API and worker runtime image remained ai-platform:2384e19-context-source-provenance with matching runtime subject labels.

The smoke verified ordinary-user Admin Runtime denial, same-tenant admin access, G6 governance schema visibility, required tool/skill/memory domains, implemented control signals, trimmed skill dashboard contract output, and no forbidden projection terms in the reviewed summary.

Policy and contract text may name forbidden payload classes as denylist labels; that is distinct from exposing private payload fields or credential-shaped values, and both the allowed-label and private-value cases are covered by verifier regression tests.

This evidence supports G6/Admin Runtime governance projection acceptance only. It does not close G6 ordinary-user UX acceptance, signed package or SBOM review evidence, office context-pack runtime injection, production capacity, Docker sandbox hardening, ordinary-user multi-agent exposure, G9 runtime export, or packaged frontend image release gates.
open_followups: Legacy frontend route remap or policy enforcement remains required before ordinary-user governance acceptance.
Skill release governance still needs signed package or SBOM review evidence plus dependency vulnerability and license evidence.
Memory governance still needs runtime context-pack persistence, executor injection, document-centric follow-up state, and frontend context provenance acceptance.
#21 recorded multi-tenant capacity/load evidence remains required before raising production concurrency defaults.
G7 Docker sandbox hardening and Docker-capable sandbox smoke remain blocked.
G8 ordinary-user multi-agent exposure remains feature-flagged/internal only.
G9 release-evidence runtime export and retention runtime acceptance remain open.
Packaged frontend image smoke/release acceptance remains separate from this thin-shell POC smoke.
